description: This figure presents room-temperature tensile stress-strain curves of Fe–28Mn–10Al–0.8C low-density steels, with strain (in %) as the horizontal axis and stress (in MPa) as the vertical axis. Six tested samples include three low-nitrogen (LN) parallel samples (LN1, LN2, LN3) and three high-nitrogen (HN) parallel samples (HN1, HN2, HN3). All samples show a similar linear elastic stage, with yield strength ranging from 647 MPa to 685 MPa. The LN group has an ultimate tensile strength (UTS) of ~832.5 MPa and fracture strain of 58% to 68%, while the HN group has a slightly higher UTS of ~841.5 MPa and significantly larger fracture strain of 68% to 78%. HN steels exhibit better plasticity, later fracture, and higher fracture energy consumption than LN steels. These results support the core conclusion that nitrogen addition improves the UTS, plasticity and toughness of the studied low-density steel while slightly reducing its yield strength, consistent with the grain refinement effect induced by fine AlN inclusions in HN steels.
